answers.answer: Helios Sentinel employs a multi-layered detection engine that combines signature-based matching, behavioral analysis, and ML-driven anomaly detection, processing up to 50,000 events per second (EPS) per tenant. Ingested data sources include endpoint telemetry (process events, file system changes, network connections), cloud workload logs (AWS CloudTrail, Azure Activity Log, GCP Audit Log), network flow data (NetFlow v9/IPFIX), and email gateway events. Average detection-to-alert latency is 2.3 seconds for signature matches and 18 seconds for behavioral detections. All detection rules are validated quarterly against the MITRE ATT&CK framework, achieving a 99.7% true positive rate across our top 100 rules.

Helios Security currently holds the following compliance certifications: (1) SOC 2 Type II — most recent audit completed December 2024, conducted by Deloitte; (2) ISO 27001:2022 — certified March 2024 by BSI; (3) FedRAMP Moderate — authorized June 2024, sponsored by the Department of Homeland Security (DHS); (4) HIPAA — last assessment October 2024, with a Business Associate Agreement (BAA) available; (5) PCI DSS v4.0 Level 1 Service Provider — validated September 2024 by Coalfire; and (6) StateRAMP — authorized January 2025. All certifications are maintained on a continuous monitoring basis, supplemented by quarterly internal audits. Audit reports are available upon request under NDA.

Helios Security's EPP+EDR bundle is priced per seat per month as follows: 500 endpoints at $18/seat/month ($108,000/year), 1,000 endpoints at $15/seat/month ($180,000/year, reflecting a 17% volume discount), and 5,000 endpoints at $11/seat/month ($660,000/year, reflecting a 39% volume discount). Volume discounts are automatically applied at each tier, with additional multi-year discounts of 5% for a 2-year commitment and 10% for a 3-year commitment. The minimum contract term is 12 months. Optional add-ons include SIEM (+$6/seat/month) and MDR (+$12/seat/month); all pricing excludes professional services.

Helios Security currently serves 47 customers in the financial services vertical, spanning 12 banks, 8 insurance carriers, 15 asset management firms, and 12 fintech companies, with an average NPS of 72 in this sector. Three approved reference accounts are: (1) Meridian National Bank — 3,200 endpoints running EPP+EDR+SIEM, deployed since 2022; (2) Crestview Capital Partners — 850 endpoints running EPP+MDR, deployed since 2023; (3) Apex Insurance Group — 5,100 endpoints on the full Helios platform, deployed since 2021.

Helios provides full EU data residency through dedicated infrastructure in Frankfurt (AWS eu-central-1) and Dublin (AWS eu-west-1), with a strict guarantee that customer data never leaves the selected region. All data at rest is protected with AES-256-GCM encryption, and customers may manage their own keys via AWS KMS or a Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) model. Data in transit is secured using TLS 1.3 across all API and agent communications, with additional certificate pinning enforced on endpoint agents. A GDPR-compliant Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is included in all EU contracts, and the platform undergoes annual third-party penetration testing by NCC Group; data retention is tenant-configurable, defaulting to 90 days for raw telemetry and 13 months for aggregated alerts.
sources: Helios Data Sovereignty & Privacy Whitepaper v3.1

issues.summary: The financial services customer breakdown sums to 47 (12 + 8 + 15 + 12 = 47), which is internally consistent, but the sub-count of 12 banks and 12 fintech companies is identical, which may raise reader suspicion — however the arithmetic does check out; no true inconsistency here.
issues.recommended_fix: No action required — arithmetic is correct.

review.overall_assessment: The five answers are largely internally consistent across numerical claims, dates, certifications, tone, and confidence levels. The one structural item worth a final human review is the Q4 sub-count symmetry (12 banks and 12 fintech), but it is arithmetically correct and not a genuine inconsistency. No material contradictions were identified.
